columns.description: Unique subject identifier for each individual in the study
Time of observation or event in hours from first dosing
Dependent variable; the observed tobramycin concentration in mg/L
Amount of drug administered (dose) in mg; only populated when evid = 1
Infusion rate in mg/h; used to specify IV infusion rate when evid = 1
Duration of infusion in hours; used to specify infusion duration when evid = 1
Event identification flag: 0 = observation/sample, 1 = dose event, 2 = other event, 3 = reset, 4 = reset and dose
Compartment number; identifies which compartment the dose is given to or sample is from
Missing dependent variable flag: 0 = observed value, 1 = missing value (e.g., BQL data or dosing event)
Age of the subject in years
Body weight of the subject in kg
Sex of the subject; coded as 0 = Male, 1 = Female
Creatinine clearance, a measure of renal function in mL/min
Baseline serum creatinine concentration in mg/dL at the start of treatment
Diabetes diagnosis flag: 0 = no diabetes, 1 = diabetes
Hypertension diagnosis flag: 0 = no hypertension, 1 = hypertension
Number of prior nephrotoxic antibiotic therapy courses
Individual empiric Bayesian estimate of tobramycin clearance in L/h
Individual empiric Bayesian estimate of central volume of distribution in L
Individual empiric Bayesian estimate of intercompartmental clearance in L/h
Individual empiric Bayesian estimate of peripheral volume of distribution in L
Individual random effect (ETA) on clearance; deviation from the population typical value on the log scale
Individual random effect (ETA) on central volume of distribution; deviation from the population typical value on the log scale
Individual random effect (ETA) on intercompartmental clearance; deviation from the population typical value on the log scale
Individual random effect (ETA) on peripheral volume of distribution; deviation from the population typical value on the log scale
